description_long: This object is a rectangular, reddish-brown stone block with twelve evenly spaced, shallow circular indentations on its top surface. The block has sharp, clean edges and a uniform color, suggesting it is made from fired clay or concrete. Its proportions are typical of a standard construction brick, with a length much greater than its height. The sides are smooth and unadorned, while the top surface is characterized by the rowed pattern of indentations, likely for improved mortar adhesion. There are no visible cracks, chips, or signs of wear, indicating it is new or well-maintained. The overall appearance is utilitarian, designed for structural use in urban environments.
description: A rectangular reddish-brown stone block with twelve shallow circular indentations on its top surface and smooth, clean sides. The block is uniform in color and shape, resembling a standard construction brick.
description_view_0: Front view showing the long, flat face of the block with all twelve circular indentations visible on the top.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the shorter end of the block, with the rowed indentations receding into the distance.
description_view_2: Rear view, visually identical to the front, again showing the long face and all twelve indentations.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring view 1, with the short end and the indentations aligned away from the viewer.
attribute_installation_method: mortared in place with other blocks
attribute_weathering_pattern: no visible weathering; surfaces appear clean and unworn
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: forms part of a continuous wall or pavement
